title: The Federalist Papers
author: James Madison
content: “If men were angels, no government would be necessary.”
action: Context: The justification for building redundant systems of control to manage the inevitable risks of human behavior.

title: Common Sense
author: Thomas Paine
content: “Government, even in its best state, is but a necessary evil; in its worst state, an intolerable one.”
action: Context: The assessment of the 'cost of operation' for any centralized social structure.

title: The Communist Manifesto
author: Karl Marx & Friedrich Engels
content: “The history of all hitherto existing society is the history of class struggles.”
action: Context: The identification of friction—or 'struggle'—as the primary driver of historical change.

title: Reflections on the Revolution in France
author: Edmund Burke
content: “Those who don't know history are destined to repeat it.”
action: Context: The critical necessity of utilizing historical data to avoid repeating structural errors.

title: Das Kapital
author: Karl Marx
content: “Capital is dead labour, that, vampire-like, only lives by sucking living labour.”
action: Context: A definition of economic resources as a parasitic mechanism if not actively managed.
